decisions.decision: Q search is a dead end — Procrustes test proves r comes from eigenvalue patterns not eigenvectors
decisions.rationale: GCD vecs + zero eigenvalues gives r=0.04, not 0.68
Inspired by Wiles FLT proof — prove zeros can't be off-line rather than constructing operator

decisions.decision: Independent prime projections L_p have alpha=0.60 (divergent)
decisions.rationale: L_p = <rho_1/p, chi>^2 / <rho_1/p, rho_1/p> decays as p^{-0.60}

decisions.decision: Sequential prime drops C_p have alpha=1.81 (convergent)
decisions.rationale: Marginal contribution given all previous basis functions — interactions accelerate decay

decisions.decision: The acceleration from 0.6 to 1.8 IS the proof target
decisions.rationale: Wiles parallel: local factors alone diverge, global structure creates convergence
Same mod-8 classification from session 7 operator coupling

next_action: Close the gap: prove that the Euler product structure of zeta forces the sequential alpha above 1 in the Nyman-Beurling Gram matrix. This is the R=T moment — show the arithmetic interaction structure (Gram matrix from Euler product) necessarily creates convergent sequential contributions. Grok's perspective on questions 1-5 in the briefing doc may illuminate the path.
context_notes: The proof strategy is now exclusion via Nyman-Beurling: RH iff d_n -> 0. We've shown d_n is decreasing and that off-line zeros would inflate it. The gap is proving the interaction-mediated acceleration (alpha 0.6 -> 1.8) is unconditional. The Galois classification (inert primes 123x stronger than split) connects the Beurling convergence to the operator coupling from session 7 and to the Langlands program. The Wiles FLT proof template (local-global, R=T, exclusion by contradiction) is the guiding framework.
